channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Fury defeats Wach, closes in on Anthony Joshua fight
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tyson Fury defeats Mariusz Wach by TKO, setting the stage for a highly anticipated heavyweight clash with Anthony Joshua. Read more on Punch.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fury-defeats-wach-closes-in-on-anthony-joshua-fight/

item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football

item.title: Fury not convinced Joshua will 'have the guts' to fight him
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Tyson Fury remains unconvinced that Anthony Joshua will agree to a long-awaited blockbuster British showdown, questioning whether his rival has the "guts" to face him.

item.title: Nowhere like home, Tinubu urges diaspora health workers to return
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu appeals to Nigerian medical professionals in the diaspora to return home and contribute to national development, citing his own journey.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nowhere-like-home-tinubu-urges-diaspora-health-workers-to-return/

item.title: Pirlo agrees to become Italy head coach – Report
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Andrea Pirlo has agreed to become the new head coach of the Italy national team, accepting a long-term project. Get the details on Pirlo’s appointment.

item.title: PHOTOS: Nigeria’s para powerlifting team wins Commonwealth Games medals, earns N27m
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s para powerlifting team secures medals and N27m at the 2026 Commonwealth Games. Read about their impressive performance and cash rewards.

item.title: Alonso pleased with Aston Martin upgrade as Newey targets 'respectability'
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Fernando Alonso said the performance of his Aston Martin was "much better" as the team debuted a huge upgrade package during Hungarian Grand Prix practice on Friday.

item.description: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages.

item.title: ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan dismissed over sexual misconduct allegations, sources say

item.description: Member states of the International Criminal Court (ICC) on Friday voted to dismiss Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan following allegations of sexual ⁠misconduct, diplomatic sources said.

item.title: Nothing confirms layoffs, but calls market exit rumors ‘fake news’
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: In response to a report that Nothing is planning to "exit 12 markets as global shipments decline," Nothing cofounder Akis Evangelidis said the company is "reorganizing" and laying off some of its staff, but that "the reported numbers are way overblown." Evangelidis disputed a claim about underwhelmi

item.title: Roku raises streaming hardware prices by up to $50
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Roku has increased prices across its streaming hardware, with the cheapest HD Streaming Stick now priced at $39.99 instead of $29.99, as first reported by The Desk. The price hike affects all of Roku's other devices as well, with some increasing by as much as $50. Here's a rundown of the changes: Ro

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch

item.title: Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin resigns, cites ill health

item.description: Bangladesh's president, a close ally of former premier Sheikh Hasina, resigned from the largely ceremonial role on Friday citing ill health, according to a letter seen by AFP. item.title: US accuses American of allegedly wiping his phone using a ‘duress’ password during border search
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: A U.S. citizen has asked a court to throw out the government's claim that he gave over a passcode to border authorities that wiped his phone's data, opening up fresh questions about a person's constitutional rights at the U.S. border.

item.title: NAICOM Seeks Nationwide Adoption Of Lagos Building Insurance Scheme
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The National Insurance Commission (NAICOM) has thrown its full weight behind the Lagos State Building Insurance Scheme, describing it as a landmark initiative that will strengthen risk management, improve urban resilience, deepen insurance penetration, and enhance consumer protection across Nigeria.

item.title: Kim's 59 falls one shot short of equalling PGA Tour record
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Michael Kim sensationally recorded only the 16th sub-60 score in the history of the PGA Tour as he shot a 12-under par 59 to shoot up the leaderboard at the 3M Open on Friday.

item.title: You can’t ignore Google Zero anymore
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The web and Google once had a deal: Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than it made the websites, but it worked for a long time. Now, however, the deal seems to be dead. And the [

item.title: Supreme Court Affirms Oyebanji’s APC Nomination, Dismisses Olawumi’s Appeal
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Supreme Court has affirmed the judgment of the Court of Appeal upholding the nomination of Ekiti State Governor Abiodun Oyebanji as the All Progressives Congress (APC) candidate in the June 20 governorship election. In a unanimous judgment delivered on Friday, a five-member panel of the apex cou

item.title: We’ve Cleared N40bn in Bank Debts, Paid Inherited Gratuities — Akwa Ibom Gov
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Governor Umo Eno of Akwa Ibom State has said his administration has paid gratuities inherited from previous governments dating back to 2011 and has paid off bank debts worth N40bn, as he highlighted the impact of President Bola Tinubu’s reforms on subnationals. Speaking during an appearance on

item.title: Yinka Ogunbiyi: Meet 27 year old Nigerian-American engineer behind the world’s first braid-assist robot
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Yinka Ogunbiyi is a Nigerian-American entrepreneur, biomedical engineer and Harvard-trained innovator whose invention, HaloBraid, is poised to transform the global hair-braiding industry. The post Yinka Ogunbiyi: Meet 27 year old Nigerian-American engineer behind the world’s first braid-assist

item.title: Tinubu Congratulates Ogunjimi On Election As African Accountants-General Chairman
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has congratulated the Accountant-General of the Federation, Dr. Shamseldeen Babatunde Ogunjimi, on his election as Chairman of the African Association of Accountant-Generals (AAAG). In a statement issued on Friday by his Special Adviser on Information and Strategy, Bayo Onanuga

item.title: Oluwo urges youths to pair degrees with skills
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Oluwo of Iwoland and Chancellor of Westland University, Iwo, Oba Abdulrosheed Akanbi, has described the institution as one of Nigeria’s leading private universities, saying its growing academic excellence stems from the dedication, passion and teamwork of its management and workforce. The post O

item.title: Commonwealth 2026: Nigeria wins first gold medal, creates games record
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Nigeria's Esther Nworgu delivered a remarkable performance to secure the country's first gold medal at the ongoing 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow after setting a new Games record in the women's para powerlifting lightweight event.

item.title: Enugu Air: Gov. Mbah reacts to runway incident, says safety non-negotiable
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Governor of Enugu State, Dr. Peter Mbah, has reacted to Thursday's runway incident involving the state-owned airline, Enugu Air, at Benin Airport, empathizing with the passengers and crew members over the traumatic experience. The post Enugu Air: Gov. Mbah reacts to runway incident, says safety

item.title: SpaceX is banking on a successful Starship launch to stem the stock’s bleeding
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The newest timeline has Starship due to take flight on Friday.

item.title: JOHESU dismisses protest over Calabar psychiatric hospital MD appointment
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Joint Health Sector Unions, JOHESU, and the Assembly of Healthcare Professionals at the Federal Neuro-Psychiatric Hospital, Calabar, have distanced themselves from a protest over the appointment of the hospital’s substantive Medical Director, describing the action as premature. In a statem

item.title: Airtel Expands Retail Reach with 4,000 Exclusive Stores, 200,000 Touchpoints Nationwide
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Airtel Nigeria has strengthened its nationwide retail and distribution network as part of its continued investment in enhancing service delivery and making telecommunications and digital services more accessible to millions of Nigerians. The company’s retail ecosystem comprises more than 4,000 exclu

item.title: Court Rejects Ex-Abia Commissioner’s Bid To Void Otti’s N100bn Defamation Suit
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Capital Territory High Court, Jabi, has dismissed an application by former Abia State Commissioner for Information, Eze Chikamnayo, seeking to strike out Governor Alex Otti’s N100bn alleged defamation suit on the ground that the originating processes were incompetent. Justice J. E.

item.title: Ondo Police Arrest Officers Over Viral Stop-And-Search Video
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Ondo State Police Command has arrested four police officers captured in a viral video allegedly making unprofessional and ethnically insensitive remarks during a stop-and-search operation along the Lagos-Benin Expressway. Addressing journalists on Friday, the Police Public Relations Officer (PPR

item.title: Osun 2027: PDP candidate Luqman warns against unlawful substitution of name
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The defection of Senator Olubiyi Fadeyi representing Osun Central Senatorial District from the All Progressives Congress to the Peoples Democratic Party might have hit a rock as Dr. Ibraheem Adekunle Luqman warned against any attempt to unlawfully substitute his name for the 2027 general election. T

item.title: Hamilton fastest as Ferrari dominate Hungarian GP practice
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Lewis Hamilton edged his team-mate Charles Leclerc to the fastest time in second practice as Ferrari dominated the first day of running at the Hungarian Grand Prix.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: Key Developments in Nigerian Digital Regulation and Related Global Education Trends Executive Summary This report examines three significant developments tracked

item.title: Cross River signs 25-year concession to redevelop Obudu Ranch Resort
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Cross River State Government has signed a 25-year concession agreement with Living Curation Real Estate Limited to redevelop the Obudu Ranch Resort and other tourism assets through a public-private partnership. The post Cross River signs 25-year concession to redevelop Obudu Ranch Resort appeare
